description: A calendar heatmap: one date field split into Year (columns) and Month (rows), color-shaded by an aggregated measure to reveal seasonality / month-over-year patterns.

slots.notes: Month part of the SAME bound date field (rows axis / calendar cell height).
Year part of the SAME bound date field (cols axis). Reuses Order Date at a second derivation — see hazard single-date-two-derivations.
aggregated measure driving the cell color gradient.

hazards.detail: The one [Order Date] field is used at BOTH Month (rows) and Year (cols). Against the single-date committed fixture the second temporal slot cannot bind a DISTINCT field, so fixture_bind=false — a valid, honest state. On a real dataset both slots bind the same date field via qualified keys; the false reflects the fixture's one-date shape, not a template defect.
A <style-rule element='cell'> sets fixed height keyed by name to [mn:Order Date:ok] (calendar squares). Cosmetic; it references the month instance by qualified name and rides along verbatim.
